description: Phase 1.1a: Add write_file_with_parents() default method to Fs trait
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/fs.rs to understand existing Fs trait surface
Add default method write_file_with_parents(&self, path: &Path, content: &[u8]) -> io::Result<()> that calls self.create_dir_all(parent) then self.write_file(path, content)
Verify MockFs inherits the default implementation automatically
Add unit tests for write_file_with_parents: happy path, path with no parent, nested dirs
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 1.1b: Add read_or_default<T>() and read_toml_or_default<T>() default methods to Fs trait
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/fs.rs to understand existing read methods
Add default method read_or_default<T: Default + DeserializeOwned>(&self, path) that JSON-deserializes or returns T::default() on NotFound
Add default method read_toml_or_default<T: Default + DeserializeOwned>(&self, path) that TOML-deserializes or returns T::default() on NotFound
Add serde and serde_json imports to fs.rs (verify they are already in libaipm Cargo.toml dependencies)
Add unit tests: file exists with valid JSON/TOML, file does not exist returns default, file exists with invalid content returns error
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 1.1c: Migrate lockfile/mod.rs from std::fs to &dyn Fs
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lockfile/mod.rs to understand current std::fs usage (read_to_string, write, create_dir_all)
Add fs: &dyn Fs parameter to pub fn read() and pub fn write() signatures
Replace std::fs::read_to_string with fs.read_to_string()
Replace std::fs::create_dir_all + std::fs::write with fs.write_file_with_parents()
Update all callers in crates/aipm/src/main.rs to pass &fs::Real
Add MockFs-based unit test verifying the filesystem interaction
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 1.1d: Migrate linker/link_state.rs from std::fs to &dyn Fs
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/linker/link_state.rs to understand current std::fs usage
Add fs: &dyn Fs parameter to read(), write(), add(), remove(), clear_all(), list() signatures
Replace std::fs::read_to_string + NotFound default with fs.read_toml_or_default::<State>()
Replace std::fs::create_dir_all + std::fs::write with fs.write_file_with_parents()
Update all callers in linker/ and crates/aipm/src/main.rs to pass &dyn Fs
Add MockFs-based unit tests alongside existing tempdir tests
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 1.1e: Migrate linker/gitignore.rs from std::fs to &dyn Fs
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/linker/gitignore.rs to understand current std::fs usage
Add fs: &dyn Fs parameter to public functions
Replace std::fs::read_to_string with fs.read_to_string()
Replace std::fs::create_dir_all + std::fs::write with fs.write_file_with_parents()
Update all callers to pass &dyn Fs
Add MockFs-based unit test for gitignore file operations
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 1.1f: Migrate installer/manifest_editor.rs and installer/pipeline.rs from std::fs to &dyn Fs
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/installer/manifest_editor.rs to understand current std::fs usage
Read crates/libaipm/src/installer/pipeline.rs to understand current std::fs usage
Add fs: &dyn Fs parameter to public functions in both modules
Replace std::fs::read_to_string with fs.read_to_string() and std::fs::write with fs.write_file()
Update all callers in crates/aipm/src/main.rs to pass &fs::Real
Add MockFs-based unit tests for both modules
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 1.1g: Migrate workspace/mod.rs and manifest/mod.rs from std::fs to &dyn Fs
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/workspace/mod.rs to understand current std::fs usage
Read crates/libaipm/src/manifest/mod.rs to understand current std::fs usage
Add fs: &dyn Fs parameter to public functions that use std::fs::read_to_string
Replace std::fs::read_to_string with fs.read_to_string()
Update all callers (main.rs, other modules) to pass &dyn Fs / &fs::Real
Add MockFs-based unit tests for both modules
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 1.1h: Migrate aipm/src/main.rs installed registry load to use fs.read_or_default()
steps: Read crates/aipm/src/main.rs and locate load_installed_registry (around line 656)
Replace std::fs::read_to_string + manual NotFound handling with fs.read_or_default::<InstalledRegistry>(path)?
Ensure &fs::Real is available at the call site
Verify existing tests still pass
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 1.2: Create manifest/builder.rs with toml_edit-based TOML generation
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/manifest/types.rs to understand Manifest, Package, Components structs
Read existing TOML generation paths: init.rs:187, workspace_init/mod.rs:166+275, migrate/emitter.rs:1080-1113
Create crates/libaipm/src/manifest/builder.rs with PluginManifestOpts, PluginComponentsOpts, WorkspaceManifestOpts structs
Implement build_plugin_manifest() using toml_edit::DocumentMut with [package] and optional [components] sections
Implement build_workspace_manifest() using toml_edit::DocumentMut with [workspace] section and optional header comments
Add pub mod builder to crates/libaipm/src/manifest/mod.rs
Write snapshot-style unit tests comparing output against expected TOML strings for plugin and workspace manifests
Test edge cases: optional description absent, empty component arrays, special characters in names
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 1.2b: Migrate init.rs to use manifest/builder.rs and delete generate_manifest()
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/init.rs:187-203 to capture current generate_manifest() output as snapshot
Replace generate_manifest() body with call to manifest::builder::build_plugin_manifest()
Convert DocumentMut to string via .to_string() and write via fs.write_file()
Delete the old generate_manifest() function (lines 187-203)
Verify snapshot test matches (modulo intentional formatting improvements)
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 1.2c: Migrate workspace_init/mod.rs to use manifest/builder.rs
steps: Read workspace_init/mod.rs:166-180 (generate_workspace_manifest) and capture output snapshot
Read workspace_init/mod.rs:275-290 (generate_starter_manifest) and capture output snapshot
Replace generate_workspace_manifest() with call to manifest::builder::build_workspace_manifest()
Replace generate_starter_manifest() with call to manifest::builder::build_plugin_manifest() with components
Delete old generate_workspace_manifest() and generate_starter_manifest() functions
Verify snapshot tests match for both paths
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 1.2d: Migrate migrate/emitter.rs to use manifest/builder.rs and delete PluginToml structs
steps: Read migrate/emitter.rs:1080-1110 to understand private PluginToml, PluginPackage, PluginComponents structs
Read migrate/emitter.rs:1113 generate_plugin_manifest() and capture output snapshot
Replace generate_plugin_manifest() with call to manifest::builder::build_plugin_manifest() with components
Delete private PluginToml, PluginPackage, PluginComponents structs (lines 1080-1110)
Verify snapshot test matches for emitter TOML output
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 1.3: Consolidate name validation into manifest/validate.rs with ValidationMode
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/manifest/validate.rs:14-52 to understand canonical is_valid_name + is_valid_segment
Read crates/libaipm/src/init.rs:99-128 to understand duplicate is_valid_package_name
Read crates/aipm/src/wizard.rs:211 to understand validate_marketplace_name (char-set only)
Read crates/aipm-pack/src/wizard.rs:168 to understand validate_package_name (char-set only)
Add ValidationMode enum (Strict, Interactive) to manifest/validate.rs
Extend is_valid_name() to accept ValidationMode parameter: Strict applies full rules, Interactive applies char-set only and allows empty
Update init.rs to call manifest::validate::is_valid_name(name, ValidationMode::Strict) and delete is_valid_package_name + is_valid_segment (lines 99-128)
Update aipm/wizard.rs to call manifest::validate::is_valid_name(name, ValidationMode::Interactive) and delete validate_marketplace_name
Update aipm-pack/wizard.rs to call manifest::validate::is_valid_name(name, ValidationMode::Interactive) and delete validate_package_name
Add unit tests for both validation modes including edge cases
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 1.4a: Create CliError enum for aipm binary
steps: Read crates/aipm/src/main.rs to identify all error types used in run() function and the ~15 .to_string() + io::Error::other() conversions

Create crates/aipm/src/error.rs with CliError enum containing #[from] variants for each library error type (workspace_init::Error, lint::LintError, migrate::MigrateError, installer::InstallError, linker::LinkError, io::Error, etc.)
Add mod error to crates/aipm/src/main.rs
Change run() return type from Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> to Result<(), CliError>
Replace .to_string() + io::Error::other() conversions with ? operator through #[from] conversions
Add unit tests verifying From conversions compile and work
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 1.4b: Create CliError enum for aipm-pack binary
steps: Read crates/aipm-pack/src/main.rs to identify error types used in run()
Create crates/aipm-pack/src/error.rs with CliError enum containing #[from] variants for init::InitError, io::Error
Add mod error to crates/aipm-pack/src/main.rs
Change run() return type from Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> to Result<(), CliError>
Replace manual error conversions with ? operator
Add unit tests verifying From conversions
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 2.1: Create generate/ module directory and mod.rs
steps: Create directory crates/libaipm/src/generate/
Create crates/libaipm/src/generate/mod.rs with pub mod plugin_json; pub mod marketplace; pub mod settings;
Add pub mod generate to crates/libaipm/src/lib.rs
Run cargo build --workspace to verify module structure compiles

description: Phase 2.2: Create unified plugin.json generation (generate/plugin_json.rs) and fix #356
steps: Read workspace_init/mod.rs:294 generate_plugin_json() to understand minimal output (name, version, description, author only)
Read migrate/emitter.rs:1183 generate_plugin_json_multi() to understand full output (with skills, agents, hooks, etc.)
Create crates/libaipm/src/generate/plugin_json.rs with PluginJsonOpts and PluginJsonComponents structs
Implement generate_plugin_json(opts, components: Option<&PluginJsonComponents>) that builds a serde_json::Map and serializes with trailing newline
When components is Some, include skills/agents/mcp_servers/hooks/output_styles/lsp_servers/extensions arrays
Update workspace_init/mod.rs:294 to call generate::plugin_json::generate_plugin_json(opts, Some(components)) with starter plugin skill+agent — THIS FIXES #356
Update migrate/emitter.rs:1183 to call generate::plugin_json::generate_plugin_json(opts, Some(components))
Delete old generate_plugin_json() from workspace_init/mod.rs and generate_plugin_json_multi() from emitter.rs
Add unit tests: with components, without components, empty component arrays, special characters
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 2.3: Create unified marketplace.json read-modify-write (generate/marketplace.rs)
steps: Read migrate/registrar.rs:10 register_plugins() to understand Rust marketplace.json RMW with dedup check
Read workspace_init/mod.rs:477 generate_marketplace_json() to understand init-time marketplace creation
Create crates/libaipm/src/generate/marketplace.rs with PluginEntry struct
Implement register_plugin(fs, path, entry) that reads marketplace.json (or creates default), checks for duplicate by name, adds/updates entry, writes back
Implement unregister_plugin(fs, path, plugin_name) that removes entry by name
Update migrate/registrar.rs to call generate::marketplace::register_plugin() in a loop
Update workspace_init/mod.rs:477 to call generate::marketplace::register_plugin()
Add unit tests: register new plugin, register duplicate (update), unregister existing, unregister nonexistent, create from scratch
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 2.4: Create unified settings.json read-modify-write (generate/settings.rs)
steps: Read workspace_init/adaptors/claude.rs:61 to understand Rust settings.json handling (extraKnownMarketplaces + enabledPlugins)
Create crates/libaipm/src/generate/settings.rs
Implement add_known_marketplace(fs, settings_path, marketplace_path) that reads/creates settings.json and adds to extraKnownMarketplaces array
Implement enable_plugin(fs, settings_path, plugin_name) that adds to enabledPlugins array
Both functions handle file-not-exists by creating default structure
Update workspace_init/adaptors/claude.rs:61 to call generate::settings functions instead of inline JSON manipulation
Add unit tests: add marketplace to empty file, add to existing, add duplicate (no-op), enable plugin
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 2.5: Unify YAML frontmatter parsing — migrate/skill_common.rs delegates to frontmatter.rs
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/frontmatter.rs:39 to understand canonical parser (returns Frontmatter with all fields, line numbers, body)
Read crates/libaipm/src/migrate/skill_common.rs:13 to understand independent parser (returns ArtifactMetadata with name, description, hooks, disable-model-invocation)
Identify all callers of skill_common.rs parse functions in migrate/
Refactor callers to use frontmatter::parse() and extract the 4 needed fields (name, description, hooks, disable-model-invocation) from the Frontmatter result
Delete the independent parsing logic from skill_common.rs (or delete skill_common.rs entirely if no non-parsing helpers remain)
Add tests verifying the migrated callers produce identical ArtifactMetadata from frontmatter::parse()
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 3.1a: Verify check_file() parity with check() across all lint rules
steps: Run grep -r 'fn check(' crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/ to count exact number of rules implementing check()
Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/discovery.rs discover_features() to verify it classifies all file types that scan.rs would find
Run the full test suite: cargo test --workspace to confirm all existing tests pass
Compare check() and check_file() outputs by examining test coverage for each rule
Document any discrepancies found between check() and check_file() results
If discrepancies exist, fix check_file() implementations to match check() before proceeding

description: Phase 3.1b: Remove check() from Rule trait and delete scan.rs
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/rule.rs to understand current Rule trait with both check() and check_file()
Remove the check() method from the Rule trait definition in rule.rs
Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/mod.rs to find the check() dispatch path and remove it
Delete all check() implementations from each lint rule file (18 rules)
Delete crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/scan.rs entirely
Remove mod scan from crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/mod.rs
Remove any scan.rs imports from rule files
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check
Verify branch coverage is still >= 89%

description: Phase 3.2: Consolidate lint rule helpers — shared locate_json_key() and diag() in rules/mod.rs
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/hook_unknown_event.rs:21 to capture locate_json_key() implementation
Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/hook_legacy_event.rs:15 to confirm identical implementation
Move locate_json_key() to crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/mod.rs as pub(crate)
Read 4 marketplace/plugin rules to identify private diag() helpers
Create shared pub(crate) fn diag(rule_id, message, path, range, severity) -> Diagnostic in rules/mod.rs
Update hook_unknown_event.rs and hook_legacy_event.rs to import locate_json_key from mod.rs
Update 4 marketplace/plugin rules to import shared diag() from mod.rs
Delete the duplicate locate_json_key() from both hook rule files
Delete the 4 private diag() helpers from marketplace/plugin rules
Replace legacy inline MockFs in skill_missing_name.rs:125 with shared test_helpers::MockFs
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 3.3: Extract shared check_file() preamble helpers for skill, agent, and hook rules
steps: Read 7 skill rules to identify the common preamble: source_type check + frontmatter::parse()
Create pub(crate) fn parse_skill_frontmatter(content, source_type) -> Option<Frontmatter> in rules/mod.rs
Create similar parse_agent_frontmatter() and parse_hook_json() helpers for agent and hook rules
Refactor 7 skill rules to use parse_skill_frontmatter() at the top of check_file()
Refactor agent rules to use parse_agent_frontmatter()
Refactor hook rules to use parse_hook_json()
Verify all lint rule tests still pass
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 4.1: Move wizard types to libaipm behind 'wizard' feature flag
steps: Read crates/aipm/src/wizard.rs to capture PromptStep, PromptKind, PromptAnswer definitions and styled_render_config()
Read crates/aipm-pack/src/wizard.rs to confirm types are structurally identical
Add wizard feature flag to crates/libaipm/Cargo.toml: [features] wizard = ['dep:inquire'] and inquire = { workspace = true, optional = true }
Update crates/aipm/Cargo.toml and crates/aipm-pack/Cargo.toml to: libaipm = { path = '../libaipm', features = ['wizard'] }
Create crates/libaipm/src/wizard.rs gated with #[cfg(feature = 'wizard')]
Move PromptStep, PromptKind, PromptAnswer, and styled_render_config() into the new wizard.rs
Add #[cfg(feature = 'wizard')] pub mod wizard to crates/libaipm/src/lib.rs
Update crates/aipm/src/wizard.rs to import from libaipm::wizard and delete local type definitions
Update crates/aipm-pack/src/wizard.rs to import from libaipm::wizard and delete local type definitions
Delete validate_marketplace_name from aipm/wizard.rs and validate_package_name from aipm-pack/wizard.rs (replaced by manifest::validate calls)
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 4.2: Standardize error modules — move large inline error enums to dedicated error.rs files
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/mod.rs to count LintError variants and determine if 3+ (needs error.rs)
Read crates/libaipm/src/migrate/mod.rs to count error variants
Read crates/libaipm/src/workspace_init/mod.rs to count error variants
For modules with 3+ variants: create dedicated error.rs, move the error enum there, add pub mod error and pub use error::Error re-export
For lint/mod.rs: create lint/error.rs and move LintError there
For migrate/mod.rs: create migrate/error.rs if 3+ variants
For workspace_init/mod.rs: create workspace_init/error.rs if 3+ variants
For all modules touched in this spec: replace explicit .map_err(|e| Error::Variant{...}) with #[from] where it's a 1:1 conversion
Verify init.rs and cache.rs keep inline errors (1-2 variants)
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Phase 4.3: Consolidate read-or-default pattern using Fs trait helpers
steps: Read crates/aipm/src/main.rs around line 656 to verify load_installed_registry was migrated in Phase 1.1h
Read crates/libaipm/src/linker/link_state.rs:37 to verify it was migrated to fs.read_toml_or_default in Phase 1.1d
Confirm cache.rs:376 is documented as whole-module exception (no change needed)
Confirm linker/gitignore.rs:90 is documented as plain-text exception (no change needed)
Verify all B9 locations are now using shared helpers or documented as exceptions
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Integration test: aipm init followed by aipm lint produces zero diagnostics (verifies #356 fix)
steps: Create an integration test or cucumber feature that runs aipm init in a temp directory
Run aipm lint on the initialized workspace
Assert zero diagnostics are returned
Verify specifically that plugin.json contains component arrays (skills, agents) for the starter plugin
Verify that the starter plugin's SKILL.md and agent .md files have proper frontmatter
Run cargo test --workspace to verify integration test passes

description: Final verification: full workspace health check with branch coverage gate
steps: Run cargo build --workspace
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Run cargo fmt --check
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov clean --workspace
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --workspace --branch
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --doc
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch --ignore-filename-regex '(tests/|research/|specs/|wizard_tty\.rs|lsp\.rs)'
Verify TOTAL branch column shows >= 89%
If coverage dropped below 89%, add targeted tests for uncovered branches in new/refactored code
